question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of retry logic or timeout configuration in RecordFileServiceImpl.findByBlockType() during a network partition, causing the underlying Spring Data JPA call to block on a TCP connection that is silently dropped by a network device, holding a thread for the full socket timeout duration (potentially minutes)?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByTimestamp() accepts a Long (nullable) parameter, and if a null is passed (e.g., via a JSON-RPC call with a missing block parameter), the unboxing to primitive long in RecordFileRepository.findByTimestamp(long timestamp) throws NullPointerException during a partition where input validation may be bypassed due to error handling changes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the split between CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST (500ms TTL) and CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX (10min TTL) during partition recovery, where findLatest() returns a new post-partition block but findByIndex() for the same index still returns the pre-partition cached RecordFile, causing inconsistent responses between eth_blockNumber and eth_getBlockByNumber?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileServiceImpl has no health check or partition detection logic, so during a partition the service continues to accept and process requests, returning stale or empty results without any error indication, causing users to silently receive incorrect blockchain state data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'unless = #result == null' cache condition in RecordFileRepository.findByTimestamp() during a partition, where DB unavailability causes an exception (not null) that bypasses the unless condition, potentially caching an error state or causing the @CachePut to write a null/invalid index entry?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'unless = #result == null' condition on findEarliest() and findLatest() to observe that during a partition these methods return null (DB error), which is NOT cached, causing every subsequent request to hit the partitioned DB and amplify the outage impact through repeated failed DB connections?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockType.of() with an empty string returns BlockType.LATEST, so during a partition where findLatest() is stale, any API endpoint that defaults to an empty block parameter will silently use stale chain-tip data without the user being aware of the partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cross-cache write (@CachePut to CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX) in findByTimestamp() during a partition recovery race, where two concurrent requests for different timestamps both resolve to the same RecordFile index but write different RecordFile objects to the index cache, causing non-deterministic block data responses?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maximumSize=1 constraint of CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST during a partition, where a single request that populates the cache with a stale 'latest' block prevents all other concurrent requests from getting a fresh DB result for the full 500ms TTL, effectively serializing all latest-block queries through a single stale value?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send a timestamp corresponding to a transaction that was in-flight during the network partition, causing findByTimestamp() to return Optional.empty() (no RecordFile contains that timestamp), and if the caller treats empty as 'transaction not found' rather than 'partition gap', the user can determine which transactions were lost during the partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of monotonicity enforcement in RecordFileServiceImpl.findByBlockType() during a partition, where the 'latest' block number decreases after partition recovery (if the DB rolls back), causing the service to return a lower block number than previously returned, breaking client-side chain tracking that assumes block numbers are monotonically increasing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the expireAfterAccess semantics of CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_TIMESTAMP by sending periodic keep-alive requests for a stale timestamp entry during a partition, preventing the 10-minute access expiry from triggering and keeping the stale RecordFile in cache indefinitely after partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to observe timing differences in findByBlockType() response times (fast=cache hit, slow=DB query/timeout), allowing the user to fingerprint which block numbers are cached and infer the query patterns of other users on the system?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause findByTimestamp() to return a RecordFile whose consensusEnd is from a DB replica that is behind the primary, causing the EVM to execute contract calls against a state that does not reflect the most recent transactions, leading to incorrect balance or storage reads?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of 10000 unique timestamp values to findByTimestamp() just before a network partition, filling the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_TIMESTAMP cache (maximumSize=10000) with pre-partition stale data, so that all timestamp lookups during the partition window are served from stale cache without any DB errors being surfaced?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByBlockType() for a numeric block calls recordFileRepository.findByIndex(block.number()), and during a partition where the DB is unavailable, the Caffeine cache for CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX serves a stale RecordFile, causing the user to receive block data that does not match the current DB state after recovery?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of connection timeout configuration visible in RecordFileServiceImpl to cause findByBlockType() to hold a database connection open indefinitely during a partition, consuming connection pool resources and preventing other service components from accessing the database?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network partition to cause a split-brain between CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST (500ms TTL, returns post-partition latest) and CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX (10min TTL, returns pre-partition data for same index), causing eth_getBlockByNumber('latest') and eth_getBlockByNumber(N) to return different RecordFile objects for the same block number N?
